The ceiling looks like folded stone until one patch peels loose and falls. More shapes stir above as the first darkmantle smothers the nearest light.
Old webbing narrows a side passage where husks hang between stalagmites. The spider waits beyond the bend, using every vibration in the stone.
A wet shine creeps down the wall beside a mineral vein. By the time the smell of acid reaches the party, the ooze is already sliding across the floor.
A dropped stone returns three different echoes from the dark ahead. Reading the timing reveals a safer passage through the branching chamber.
Pebbles rattle away underfoot and the whole slope begins to move. The slide is short, but jagged enough to punish anyone caught off balance.
Cold water drips into a basin of pale stone, filtered through layers of mineral and root. Washing wounds there stings sharply, then leaves them cleaner.
A faint tapping answers from behind a collapsed wall. The pattern is deliberate, and the air on the other side will not last forever.
A lantern still burns blue beside bootprints that end at a blank wall. Someone has vanished through a passage that only opens in certain echoes.
A thin crack in the stone sings whenever wind moves through it. Locals want the sound silenced before it draws something from deeper caverns.
Pale mushrooms grow where old wood has rotted into the cave floor. Several are edible, but a bruised cap turns black if handled roughly.
Blue lichen lights a damp ledge just beyond easy reach. Scraping it cleanly preserves enough glow for later use.
A dry pocket beneath an old roost holds sharp-smelling guano useful to gardeners and alchemists. Stirring it too hard fills the tunnel with choking dust.
Small white fish drift through a clear pool, sensing ripples more than light. A quiet hand can net them before the whole school dives under stone.
Flat black stones hide pale crayfish with oversized claws. The catch is good if the water is not muddied too quickly.
A narrow current carries slick eels from one dark pool to another. Blocking the run for too long risks flooding the passage behind it.
A red-brown vein cuts across the cave wall where the stone is already cracked. Careful strikes free ore without waking the ceiling.
A pocket of clear quartz sparkles behind a thin sheet of limestone. The best crystals can be teased out before the pocket collapses.
White nodules bead along a dry tunnel where bitter air leaks from below. Breaking them loose fills the mouth with mineral dust.
Abandoned shoring still props up a side shaft, but some beams can be salvaged without weakening the passage. Choosing the wrong one invites trouble.
Tall, woody fungi grow in a damp chamber where no trees could live. Their hollow stalks make light poles if cut above the root mat.
Tree roots hang through a ceiling crack in thick braided cords. Dead sections can be cut into usable lashings without harming the living roots.
Goblinoid voices bounce from three tunnels at once, hiding the real attackers. A rough wolf banner is painted on the stone in fresh soot.
The cave narrows into wet curtains of stone where a shed mule harness hangs from a spike. The grick uncoils from above when light crosses the threshold.
A yellow smear creeps along a fork in the tunnel, almost invisible against sulfur-stained rock. The first blade that cuts it makes the hazard worse.
Old dwarven survey notches mark safe footing around a sinkhole. Following their sequence avoids hours of false passages.
The torch flame gutters blue as the tunnel dips into stale air. A quick retreat keeps lungs clear before panic sets in.
Warm mineral water beads from a copper-green wall. A short rest beside it eases stone-bruised muscles and raw throats.
A loaded mule stands trembling at a cave mouth, its lead rope cut and its packs full of assay tools. The owner's bootprints vanish inside.
A mine charter sealed by a frontier town lies among goblin scraps. Recovering the matching claim-stones could stop a quiet land theft.
Someone whispers from behind a waterfall deep in the cave, giving directions only when no weapon is drawn. The voice knows a prisoner is still alive.
A stone door bears fresh pry marks and an old warning against opening it during a red moon. Something inside has started hammering back.
A soot-black map shows three tunnels to an abandoned forge, but one route is crossed out with the word 'hungry'. The ink is only hours old.
Pale tubers grow in cracks where bat guano warms the stone. They are edible if cut away from bitter black roots.
Soft glowcaps line a damp shelf and dim when touched. The brightest caps make safe trail markers if lifted whole.
Sharp-scented herbs sprout beneath a bat roost where warm air rises. Gathering them quietly keeps the ceiling asleep.
White trout drift in a black pool fed by underground springs. They feel vibration through the water faster than hooks can move.
Crayfish hide beneath flat stones in a cold pool. Each lifted rock sends silt through the water and hides the rest.
A slick eel has claimed an abandoned mine sluice and snaps at anything shiny. Catching it means blocking both exits first.
Green staining reveals a copper seam behind loose shale. A few careful blows free ore before the wall flakes apart.
Milky stones glitter inside a narrow pocket that only catches light from below. The pocket is fragile and easy to collapse.
A rib of salt crystals runs along a dry chamber wall. Clean pieces can be snapped away if damp hands stay clear.
An abandoned brace has cracked but not rotted. Removing the good beam without bringing down loose stone takes careful wedges.
A broken mine cart lies under dust and bat droppings. Its sideboards are old, dry, and useful if pried free without splitting.
A woody fungal stalk rises from a cavern floor like a pale tree. Cut sections dry into light, corky planks.
